storage_policy.refresh_policy: registered producers refresh after successful completion

entries.title: Historical Host MLIR AOT GEMM Benchmark
entries.claim_boundary: Host CPU AOT correctness and timing evidence from the earlier generic Transform pipeline; it is not the Chapter 3 frozen dataset and does not demonstrate BLIS replacement.

paper_use.decision: Use only to document the earlier generic Transform/AOT pipeline. Use Chapter 3 for frozen preliminary statistics.
usable_claims: The host MLIR Transform-to-AOT path compiled, ran, and checked correctness.

entries.title: Historical Native C++ Tile Sanity Benchmark
entries.claim_boundary: A native C++ schedule sanity check retained for historical comparison; it is not primary MLIR performance evidence.

paper_use.decision: Retain as a native C++ sanity check; do not use for the MLIR method's primary performance claim.
usable_claims: Early native tiling variants were executable and measurable.

entries.title: Historical QEMU Scalar and RVV Correctness Check
entries.claim_boundary: Scalar and RVV emulator correctness evidence only; no real-board performance conclusion is supported.

paper_use.decision: Correctness-only emulator evidence is outside the current x86 BLIS thesis evaluation.
usable_claims: Scalar and RVV test executables passed the recorded QEMU correctness check.

entries.title: Historical RVV Toolchain and Instruction-generation Check
entries.claim_boundary: RVV toolchain and instruction-generation evidence only; it does not establish emulator correctness or real-board performance.

paper_use.decision: Toolchain evidence only; it does not support runtime performance conclusions.
usable_claims: The configured toolchain emitted recorded RVV instructions.

entries.title: Historical Transform Dialect to RVV Code-generation Demo
entries.claim_boundary: Transform and RVV code-generation evidence; fixed-size MLIR vectors and generated RVV instructions do not by themselves establish target-hardware speedup.

paper_use.decision: Keep as code-generation evidence; fixed vectors and RVV instructions do not establish target speedup.
usable_claims: Transform Dialect generated the recorded RVV-oriented IR and object code.

entries.title: CGO 2025 Transform Dialect Section 4.5 Core Materials
entries.claim_boundary: The extracted files document the Section 4.5 tuner interface and reusable patterns, but missing support files prevent claiming a standalone local reproduction or observed library replacement.

paper_use.decision: Use as a source-grounded interface reference, not as a local reproduction or local measurement.
usable_claims: The extracted Section 4.5 materials expose a reusable parameterized Transform and tuner interface pattern.

entries.title: Chapter 3 GEMM Expert Knowledge and Preliminary MLIR Evidence
entries.claim_boundary: Automated artifacts, the preliminary MLIR AOT path, and all 16 source-backed thesis-author signoffs are complete. All correct Transform candidates were still observed fallback rather than BLIS replacement.

paper_use.decision: Primary evidence for Fact/Contract/Rule modeling and the eight-shape preliminary MLIR study. All 16 source-backed review cases have explicit thesis-author signoff.
usable_claims: The extractor produced source-linked facts and contracts with explicit evidence and review state.
The eight-shape preliminary MLIR/AOT dataset quantifies legal-space reduction, fallback, correctness, and compile cost.

entries.title: Chapter 4 Candidate Space to Chapter 5 BLIS Evaluator Integration
entries.claim_boundary: A six-evaluation integration smoke test on one x86-64 AVX2/FMA host. It proves that Chapter 4 Expert+Shell candidates and unmodified BaCO callbacks drive the Chapter 5 real BLIS direct/adapt/fallback evaluator, but it is not the formal multi-space, multi-seed Chapter 6 comparison.

paper_use.decision: Primary integration evidence that Chapter 4 candidates and BaCO callbacks now drive observed Chapter 5 direct/adapt/fallback execution. The six-point tuning data is not a formal convergence comparison.
usable_claims: Checker-predicted direct, adapt, and fallback cases match observed runtime paths.
The integrated BaCO callback reaches real BLIS microkernel evaluations with candidate-level correctness and path evidence.

entries.title: Chapter 4 Microkernel-aware Schedule-space Experiment Package
entries.claim_boundary: The method and tuner loop are complete. Predicted direct/adapt paths remain observed fallback because no real BLIS packing or library-call adapter is present.

paper_use.decision: Primary method evidence for B1-B4 space construction, Checker decisions, provenance, complexity, and BaCO interface. Its old path examples remain observed fallback.
usable_claims: The implementation generates Generic, Contract-only, Expert-core, and Expert+Shell spaces with explicit provenance.
The six-task filtering funnel and generation-overhead measurements are reproducible.
Unmodified BaCO consumed the exported finite lookup in a bounded smoke run.

entries.title: Chapter 5 Real BLIS Packing and Microkernel Call Adapter
entries.claim_boundary: Rank-2 row-major f32 static direct, M/N/K fringe adapt, and one-binary dynamic-shape execution now perform real BLIS-compatible packing and call bli_sgemm_haswell_asm_6x16. Multi-shape speedups use a scalar-loop dynamic fallback; arbitrary layouts, additional dtypes/contracts, optimized dynamic baselines, parallel execution, and cross-host statistics remain outside this evidence.

paper_use.decision: Primary evidence for real packing, BLIS 6x16 invocation, fringe adaptation, and dynamic shape correctness. Performance remains preliminary because the dynamic baseline is scalar-loop.
usable_claims: The generated binary contains and executes the real BLIS Haswell f32 6x16 microkernel path.
Seven dynamic shapes validate direct/adapt execution, nonzero-C accumulation, and runtime fringe counts.
Reported speedups are only relative to the declared scalar-loop dynamic fallback.

entries.title: Chapter 6 Formal Kernel-aware GEMM Evaluation
entries.claim_boundary: Formal B1-B4 equal-budget, five-seed evaluation over 36 predeclared row-major f32 shapes on one single-thread x86-64 AVX2/FMA host, with optimized dynamic MLIR and complete BLIS references, ablations, shape holdout, and overhead. BaCO uses offline replay over real measured candidates. Confidence intervals do not represent cross-host variation; additional dtype/architecture performance remains outside this completed run. Chapter 3 author signoff is complete and separately recorded.

paper_use.decision: Primary formal evidence for RQ1-RQ6 within the single-host row-major f32 boundary. Use its B1-B4 comparison, complete-BLIS reference, ablations, holdout, overhead, and confidence intervals; do not extend the result to additional dtypes or hosts.
usable_claims: Thirty-six predeclared shapes and eight core tuning shapes provide the formal space-quality and baseline matrix.

B1-B4 use equal 25-candidate budgets, five seeds, one real measurement corpus, and pinned BaCO 3.0; finite domains, seeds, and objective values are unchanged, while runtime compatibility interventions are disclosed separately.
The evaluation includes a genuinely vectorized dynamic MLIR reference, complete BLIS bli_sgemm, component ablations, shell sensitivity, zero-shot holdout, and system overhead.
